api: Whisky Hunter public API (OpenAPI/Swagger 2.0 spec at https://whiskyhunter.net/api/?format=openapi)
scope: Pass B full-surface expansion, re-confirmed against the live drf-yasg-generated OpenAPI 2.0 document (https://whiskyhunter.net/api/?format=openapi, fetched 2026-07-03) and every live JSON response it documents. The real Whisky Hunter public API publishes EXACTLY 5 GET endpoints, all read-only and unauthenticated: this bundle now covers all 5 (the aggregate-across-all list stream, the 2 metadata list streams, and the 2 per-slug detail streams reached via fan_out over the metadata lists' slugs). There is no write capability of any kind (the Basic securityDefinition declared in the OpenAPI doc is a drf-yasg template artifact for the underlying Django admin site, never a real credentialed write path exposed to API callers -- verified live: every documented path is GET-only, no POST/PUT/PATCH/DELETE operation appears anywhere in the spec) and no other resource beyond these 5. The legacy connector's own field-shape assumption (an 'id'/'winning_bid' pair on 'auctions', an 'id' field on 'distilleries') does not match the real wire shape at all -- the live API has never returned an 'id' field on either resource; schemas and fixtures below now reflect the real, recorded response shape instead (see docs.md Known limits).
